native_text:
Khazanah Nasional Berhad ©2026  | 18
Seri Negara and Bangunan Sultan Abdul Samad ready for Visit Malaysia 2026 
as part of Khazanah's heritage conservation efforts
Led by Khazanah, the restoration of Seri Negara was completed 
within 10 months, ahead of schedule; and reopened to the public 
on 20th December 2025. Seri Negara brings to life the stories that 
have shaped our nation in an inclusive and inspiring space where 
Malaysians and visitors can discover our history and heritage.
Post an 11-month restoration programme, Block 1 of the 
Bangunan Sultan Abdul Samad Complex reopened to the public 
on 2nd February 2026. Khazanah continues to oversee tenant fit-
out works for Block 2, with opening scheduled for Q2 2026. The 
conservation journey aims to establish a vibrant arts, cultural and 
heritage hub celebrating creativity and community.
In collaboration with:
Bangunan Sultan Abdul Samad Complex Block 1 & 2
Confluence Hall
Visionary Hall
Hikayat Chamber
KL Gallery Gift Shop
Official Launch of BSAS Block 1
Official Launch of Seri Negara
Gallery 1
Seri Negara
Ruang Merdeka
Gallery 4
Grand Staircase

vision_data.slide_title: Seri Negara and Bangunan Sultan Abdul Samad ready for Visit Malaysia 2026 as part of Khazanah's heritage conservation efforts
vision_data.narrative_text:
Seri Negara
Led by Khazanah, the restoration of Seri Negarawas completed within 10 months, ahead of schedule; and reopened to the public on 20th December 2025. Seri Negara brings to life the stories that have shaped our nation in an inclusive and inspiring space where Malaysians and visitors can discover our history and heritage.

Bangunan Sultan Abdul Samad Complex Block 1 & 2
Post an 11-month restoration programme, Block 1 of the Bangunan Sultan Abdul Samad Complex reopened to the public on 2nd February 2026. Khazanah continues to oversee tenant fit-out works for Block 2, with opening scheduled for Q2 2026. The conservation journey aims to establish a vibrant arts, cultural and heritage hub celebrating creativity and community.
vision_data.chart_description: The slide features images of various spaces within Seri Negara and Bangunan Sultan Abdul Samad, including a grand staircase, several galleries, and event launch settings, highlighting the architectural heritage and cultural efforts.

slide_title: Seri Negara and Bangunan Sultan Abdul Samad ready for Visit Malaysia 2026 as part of Khazanah's heritage conservation efforts

Malaysia to sustain resilient growth in 2026, underpinned by strong private investment 
and FDI, even as global growth remains modest
The IMF recently upgraded Malaysia’s 2026 and 2027 GDP growth forecasts to 4.3% (an upward revision of 0.3 percentage points), suggesting Malaysia will continue to outperform the global and emerging 
economies’ average. Global growth 3.3% in 2026, 3.2% in 2027; Emerging and Developing Economies 4.2% in 2026, 4.1% in 2027
